records.ety: [Cf. F. boudin black pudding, sausage, L. botulus, botellus, a sausage, G. & Sw. pudding pudding, Dan. podding, pudding, LG. puddig thick, stumpy, W. poten, potten, also E. pod, pout, v.]

defs: A species of food of a soft or moderately hard consistence, variously made, but often a compound of flour or meal, with milk and eggs, etc.
quotes: And solid pudding against empty praise.

defs: Anything resembling, or of the softness and consistency of, pudding.
An intestine; especially, an intestine stuffed with meat, etc.; a sausage.

quotes: Eat your pudding, slave, and hold your tongue.
Mars, that still protects the stout, In pudding time came to his aid.
